title: Cutting assembly
abstract: This disclosure relates to a cutting assembly for a rock excavation machine with integrated sensors. The rock excavation machine comprises a base unit, one or more moveable support arms extending from the base unit, a drive spindle rotatably mounted to the or each moveable support arm, and a disk cutter fixed about the drive spindle such that rotation of the drive spindle causes a corresponding rotation of the disk cutter.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A cutting assembly for a rock excavation machine comprising: a base unit, one or more moveable support arms extending from the base unit, a drive spindle rotatably mounted to the or each moveable support arm, a disk cutter fixed about the drive spindle such that rotation of the drive spindle causes a corresponding rotation of the disk cutter, the disk cutter comprising a cutter body and one or more cutting elements arranged peripherally around the cutter body, the cutting assembly further comprising one or more sensors for detecting one or more conditions of the cutting elements and/or of the surrounding environment. 2. A cutting assembly as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising a wireless signal transmitting module connected to the one or each sensor. 3. A cutting assembly as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising a control unit for the acquisition and processing of signals generated by the or each sensor into data, and a communication unit for transmitting data relating to the or each condition. 4. A cutting assembly as claimed in claim 3, wherein the communication unit is configured to transmit data from the control unit to a data harvesting system using wireless communication technology. 5. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, in which at least one sensor is embedded or integrated into the cutter body. 6. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, in which at least one sensor is located in or on one or more of the cutting elements. 7. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a tool holder for the or each cutting element, and in which at least one sensor is located in or on the tool holder. 8. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the or each sensor is selected from any of the following: a temperature sensor, a pressure sensor, an X-ray sensor, a gamma ray sensor, an accelerometer. 9. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the or each sensor is a sensor configured to monitor the chemistry of the cutting conditions. 10. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the or each sensor is a sensor to identify the rock formation or materials for extraction. 1 1. A cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a vision camera system for visually monitoring the or each condition and/or to identify the rock formation or materials for extraction. 12. A real-time excavation monitoring system comprising a cutting assembly as claimed in any preceding claim and a data harvesting system. 13. A real-time excavation monitoring system as claimed in claim 12, further comprising a data analysis package. 14. A real-time excavation monitoring system as claimed in claim 12 or 13, in which the data analysis package is in-line with the cutting assembly. 15. A real-time excavation monitoring system as claimed in claim 12 or 13, in which the data analysis package is remote from the cutting assembly.
